text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
Stay hungry, stay foolish.
The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
Premature optimization is the root of all evil.
First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it is bad.
Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
The best error message is the one that never shows up.
Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
Walking on water and developing software from a specification are easy if both are frozen.
There are only two hard things in computer science: cache invalidation and naming things.
It always seems impossible until it is done.
The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
Whether you think you can or you think you cannot, you are right.
In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
Life is what happens when you are busy making other plans.
The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
Do not watch the clock. Do what it does. Keep going.
Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
You miss one hundred percent of the shots you do not take.
Imagination is more important than knowledge.
Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
The unexamined life is not worth living.
We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
The mind is everything. What you think you become.
An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
Quality is not an act, it is a habit.
The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
If you cannot explain it simply, you do not understand it well enough.
Genius is one percent inspiration and ninety-nine percent perspiration.
It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
Our greatest weakness lies in giving up. The most certain way to succeed is always to try just one more time.
The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
Be the change that you wish to see in the world.
Two roads diverged in a wood, and I took the one less traveled by, and that has made all the difference.
I have not failed. I have just found ten thousand ways that will not work.
A person who never made a mistake never tried anything new.
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
What we know is a drop, what we do not know is an ocean.
If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.
The computer was born to solve problems that did not exist before.
Software is a great combination between artistry and engineering.
Before software can be reusable it first has to be usable.
Try not to become a person of success, but rather try to become a person of value. Look deep into nature, and then you will understand everything better.
Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated. Everything has beauty, but not everyone sees it. The one who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.

Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight. Walking on water and developing software from a specification are easy, so long as both are frozen.
Sometimes life is going to hit you in the head with a brick. Do not lose faith. I am convinced that the only thing that kept me going was that I loved what I did.
We cannot solve our problems with the same thinking we used when we created them. A clever person solves a problem; a wise person avoids it. The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.

Your time is limited, so do not waste it living someone else's life. Do not be trapped by dogma, which is living with the results of other people's thinking. Do not let the noise of others' opinions drown out your own inner voice. And most important, have the courage to follow your heart and intuition.

It is not the critic who counts; not the one who points out how the strong stumble, or where the doer of deeds could have done them better. The credit belongs to the one who is actually in the arena, whose face is marred by dust and sweat and blood, who strives valiantly and comes up short again and again.

Programming is not about typing, it is about thinking. The best programmers are not marginally better than merely good ones. They are an order of magnitude better, measured by conceptual creativity, speed, ingenuity of design, and the sheer ability to solve hard problems cleanly.

Controlling complexity is the essence of computer programming. The cheapest, fastest, and most reliable components are those that are not there. Simplicity is a prerequisite for reliability, and a program that produces incorrect results twice as fast is infinitely slower than one that is simply correct.

Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you did not do than by the ones you did do. So throw off the bowlines, sail away from the safe harbor, and catch the trade winds in your sails. Explore. Dream. Discover.
